title: U.S. Corn Belt Drought → Corn Spike
summary: Extreme heat and drought across the U.S. Corn Belt in summer 2012 caused the USDA to sharply cut crop forecasts, driving corn to multi-year highs.
mechanism: Drought and extreme heat cut expected corn yields materially. USDA revised ending stocks sharply lower, tightening the supply/demand balance and repricing corn higher.
causal_chain: drought and extreme heat hit Corn Belt from June through August
USDA July WASDE cuts expected corn crop by ~12%
ending stocks projected near multi-year lows
corn futures reprice higher as supply deficit becomes priced in
livestock and food processing margins compress

forecast.note: Bullish corn while drought conditions persist and USDA continues revisions lower

outcome.realized_note: Corn rallied to near $8.31/bushel in August 2012, a multi-year high. USDA confirmed 12% crop cut and historic drought exposure.
outcome.chart_note: MacroTrends corn chart shows 2012 annual high at approximately $8.31/bushel
outcome.reversal_note: Prices normalized as the 2013 crop improved

title: Brazil Frost → Arabica Coffee Spike
summary: Severe frost hit Brazil's Minas Gerais coffee belt in July 2021, damaging supply expectations for the world's largest arabica producer and driving a sharp price spike.
mechanism: Frost events in Brazil's main coffee-growing regions damage the standing crop and reduce multi-season production expectations, tightening global arabica availability.
causal_chain: severe frost hits Minas Gerais coffee belt
crop damage reduces immediate and forward arabica output
traders reprice future arabica availability sharply higher
ICO reports daily arabica prices surge ~25% in one week

forecast.note: Bullish arabica; severity of frost determines multi-season magnitude

outcome.realized_note: Arabica prices rose ~25.4% in one week from 165.64 to 207.8 US cents/lb per ICO-referenced reporting. 2021 MacroTrends high ~$2.50/lb.
outcome.chart_note: MacroTrends coffee chart shows strong 2021 uptrend; annual high near $2.50/lb

sources.title: Coffee prices reach seven-year high due to concerns over frost in Brazil
Prices for arabica coffee in July 2021 surged to highest levels since November 2014

title: 1997–98 El Niño → Sugar and Cocoa Supply Disruption
summary: The severe 1997–98 El Niño caused drought in SE Asia and Australia, damaging sugar and palm oil output, and wet conditions in West Africa affected cocoa.
mechanism: El Niño disrupts rainfall patterns across multiple producing regions simultaneously, compressing supply from several commodity exporters at once.
causal_chain: El Niño declared; drought signal in SE Asia and Australia
sugar and palm oil yields in affected regions fall short
West Africa cocoa crop affected by abnormal rainfall
global inventories tighten across multiple soft commodities
prices spike on coincident supply stress

forecast.note: Bullish sugar, palm oil, cocoa during confirmed El Niño; magnitude depends on geographic footprint

outcome.realized_note: Sugar and cocoa prices elevated in 1997-98. The 1997 El Niño was one of the strongest on record.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — price confirmation needed from commodity charts for 1997-98 period
outcome.priced_in_note: Weather forecasters flagged El Niño early; some supply concern already priced before crop data confirmed
outcome.reversal_note: La Niña follow-on in 1998-99 partially reversed supply concerns

title: Australian Drought → Wheat Export Shortfall
summary: Severe drought across Australia's wheat belt in 2006 caused one of the worst wheat harvests in decades, removing a major exporter from the market and tightening global supplies.
mechanism: Australia is one of the world's largest wheat exporters. A severe domestic drought cuts its exportable surplus, tightening global trade flows and forcing importers to compete for alternative supplies.
causal_chain: prolonged drought reduces Australian grain-belt rainfall
ABARES cuts crop estimate to near-record low
Australian wheat export volumes fall sharply
global importers compete for supplies from other origins
wheat futures reprice higher

forecast.note: Bullish wheat when Australian production estimates fall; impact strongest on international wheat trade pricing

outcome.realized_note: Australia's 2006-07 wheat crop was approximately 9.8 million tonnes, down from ~25 million the prior year. Global wheat prices elevated into 2007-08.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — wheat price chart confirms 2006-07 upward trend into the 2007-08 global food crisis
outcome.reversal_note: 2008-09 record global wheat harvests eventually eased prices

title: U.S. Midwest Flooding → Corn Planting Delays
summary: Record spring flooding across the U.S. Midwest in 2019 caused historically late corn planting, shrinking the expected crop size and lifting futures.
mechanism: Flooded fields delay or prevent planting; late-planted corn has lower yield potential and faces higher risk of early frost. USDA planting progress data showing historic delays triggers futures repricing.
causal_chain: excessive rainfall and Mississippi basin flooding saturate Corn Belt fields
USDA weekly planting progress shows record-low corn planted share by late May
prevented planting claims rise; crop insurance acreage falls out of production
corn production estimates cut; prices spike on supply concern

forecast.note: Bullish corn during peak planting delay concern; magnitude depends on final prevented-planting acreage

outcome.realized_note: Corn futures rallied ~30% from April lows to June 2019 peak. USDA confirmed record prevented-planting acres.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — corn chart shows sharp spring 2019 rally
outcome.reversal_note: Prices partially retraced as final crop came in better than feared; South American supplies provided some offset

sources.title: USDA Weekly Crop Progress Reports, Spring 2019

title: Pakistan Flooding → Cotton Supply Shock
summary: Catastrophic flooding across Pakistan in 2010 submerged major cotton-growing areas, disrupting output from the world's fourth-largest cotton producer.
mechanism: Pakistan is a top global cotton exporter. Flooding of its cotton belt reduced crop yield forecasts sharply, tightening global cotton supply at a time when demand from Asian mills was strong.
causal_chain: monsoon rains produce unprecedented flooding across Pakistan
cotton fields inundated in key growing provinces
production forecast cut significantly
global cotton inventories tighten
cotton futures spike

forecast.note: Bullish cotton; coincided with already-tight global inventories and Chinese demand

outcome.realized_note: Cotton prices surged to near record highs above $2/lb in early 2011, driven by Pakistan floods plus tight global stocks.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — cotton chart confirms 2010-11 spike to multi-decade highs
outcome.priced_in_note: China demand surge was a concurrent factor amplifying the supply shock
outcome.reversal_note: Prices collapsed in 2011 as crop recovered and demand softened

title: Brazil Drought → Sugar and Coffee Supply Concern
summary: Brazil's south-central sugar belt experienced its worst drought in nearly 90 years in early 2021, reducing sugarcane yields and raising concerns for coffee and other crops.
mechanism: Brazil dominates global sugar and coffee exports. Drought in its south-central region reduces cane yields and juice content, compressing exportable surplus and repricing futures.
causal_chain: driest conditions in nearly 90 years hit Brazil south-central belt
UNICA reports below-average crushing volumes and lower ATR (sugar content)
global sugar and coffee supply estimates cut
prices rally on tighter supply outlook

forecast.note: Bullish sugar and arabica; compound effect as drought preceded the July 2021 frost

outcome.realized_note: Sugar prices rose materially in 2021. Effect was amplified by the July frost on coffee. UNICA confirmed below-trend crush.

title: European Heatwave → French and German Wheat Crop Cuts
summary: An intense summer heatwave across northern and central Europe in 2018 cut wheat yields in France and Germany, reducing EU export competitiveness.
mechanism: France and Germany are major EU wheat exporters. A heatwave during the grain-filling stage reduces test weights and yield, cutting the EU's exportable surplus and lifting global wheat benchmarks.
causal_chain: record summer heat in northern Europe stresses grain crops during filling
French soft wheat yield estimates cut to multi-year lows
German and Baltic crops also below-average
EU exportable surplus shrinks
CBOT and Paris wheat futures reprice higher

forecast.note: Moderately bullish wheat; Black Sea origin (Russia) partially offset EU shortfall

outcome.realized_note: European wheat prices rallied. Russia and Ukraine had good crops, which partially capped the rally.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — Euronext wheat chart confirms 2018 summer price rise
outcome.priced_in_note: Russian harvest was strong in 2018, dampening the upside

title: 2023 El Niño → Cocoa and Sugar Supply Disruption
summary: The return of El Niño conditions in 2023 hit West African cocoa production (drought in Ghana and Côte d'Ivoire) and SE Asian sugar, driving cocoa to record highs.
mechanism: El Niño brings hot, dry conditions to West Africa's cocoa belt. Ghana and Côte d'Ivoire supply ~60% of global cocoa. Reduced midcrop and main crop estimates drive sustained price rallies.
causal_chain: El Niño onset reduces rainfall in West African cocoa belt
ICCO and grinder reports show below-trend mid-crop volumes
disease pressure (black pod) increases under stressed crop conditions
global cocoa deficit widens; stocks-to-use falls
cocoa futures rally to record highs

forecast.note: Strongly bullish cocoa; disease amplification typical when drought weakens crop

outcome.realized_note: Cocoa prices hit record highs above $10,000/tonne in early 2024, driven by compounding supply deficits.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — ICE cocoa chart confirms multi-decade high break in 2023-24

title: India Heatwave → Wheat Production Cut and Export Ban
summary: An unusually early and intense heatwave in India in spring 2022 sharply cut wheat yields, prompting the government to ban exports and tightening an already-stressed global market.
mechanism: India had become a significant wheat exporter. A premature heatwave during grain-filling caused a sharp yield miss. The government banned exports to protect domestic supply, compounding the global shortage from the Ukraine war.
causal_chain: record March-April temperatures hit Indian wheat belt during grain-filling
USDA and FCI cut Indian wheat production estimate by ~5-6 million tonnes
India bans wheat exports to protect food security
global wheat importers lose an alternative supplier
wheat futures sustain elevated pricing

forecast.note: Reinforced bullish wheat thesis already active from Ukraine war; removes an alternative supply source

outcome.realized_note: India's export ban and production miss compounded the Ukraine-driven wheat rally in 2022.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — wheat chart shows sustained elevated pricing in H1 2022
outcome.priced_in_note: Ukraine war supply shock was the primary driver; India heatwave was a reinforcing secondary event

title: Brazil La Niña / Drought → Sugar Yield Below Trend
summary: Dry conditions in Brazil's 2016 cane-crushing season reduced sucrose content and yields, contributing to a tighter global sugar balance after years of surplus.
mechanism: Brazil controls ~40-50% of global traded sugar. Below-trend rain reduces cane yields and sucrose content (ATR), shrinking the sugar extraction per tonne of cane and tightening the supply/demand balance.
causal_chain: below-average rainfall in São Paulo state reduces cane irrigation
UNICA mid-season data shows below-prior-year ATR
Brazilian output estimates cut
global deficit narrative strengthens
raw sugar prices rally

forecast.note: Moderately bullish raw sugar; Indian and Thai supply trajectories matter for magnitude

outcome.realized_note: Raw sugar prices rallied from multi-year lows in 2015 into 2016-17, helped by Brazil crop concerns and global deficit signal.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — sugar chart shows 2016 recovery from multi-year lows
outcome.priced_in_note: Market had been in surplus for years; some recovery already anticipated

title: Australian Drought 2002 → Wheat Supply Shock
summary: Australia's 2002-03 drought was one of the worst in a century, cutting wheat production from ~24 million tonnes to ~10 million tonnes, severely reducing global export availability.
mechanism: Australia is a top global wheat exporter. A halving of production removes a large share of globally traded wheat supply, forcing importers to seek alternative origins at higher prices.
causal_chain: El Niño-linked drought reduces soil moisture across eastern Australia
Australian wheat crop falls to near 10Mt from ~24Mt prior year
export availability drops by ~7-8 million tonnes
global importers scramble for U.S., Canadian, EU supply
wheat prices spike materially

forecast.note: Strongly bullish wheat; similar mechanism to 2006 Australian drought

outcome.realized_note: Australian production fell to ~10 million tonnes, one of the smallest crops in decades. Global wheat prices elevated in 2002-03.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — wheat chart confirms 2002-03 price rise
